name: omni-test-coverage
description: Find tests related to a symbol and surface targeted commands to run them; flag a coverage gap and suggest adding a test when nothing matches.
when_to_use: After (or alongside) a refactor — answers 'what targeted command should I run to verify nothing regressed?'. Falls back to suggesting a new test when the call graph and search both come up empty.
safety_notes: Read-only flow — no step writes to disk.
Suggested commands are printed; the AI / user decides whether to run them.
Never recommend a full project-wide pytest as the only option — prefer targeted tests; only recommend the full suite as a last resort with coverage_gap=true.

steps.title: Pull the suggested-tests list from the call graph
steps.purpose: Risk badge + tests/ files known to reach the symbol via call-graph + suggested pytest/jest commands. impact.v2 returns suggested_commands as ready-to-run targeted invocations (e.g. 'pytest tests/unit/test_x.py').
post_step_guidance: If suggested_tests is non-empty, prefer the targeted suggested_commands over any broader sweep.
If suggested_tests is empty, run the search_tests step next; do not jump to a full pytest yet.

failure_policy.notes: Search step can still salvage the recipe if the graph endpoint is down.

steps.title: Hybrid-search test files mentioning the symbol
steps.purpose: Catches tests that don't show up in the static call graph (e.g. dynamic dispatch, parametrised fixtures, integration tests that import the symbol via string).

failure_policy.notes: Even a partial result is enough to recommend SOME targeted test.

steps.title: Suggest adding a targeted test when no existing test is found
steps.purpose: If omni_impact.suggested_tests is empty AND tests/ search returns no relevant test file, mark coverage_gap=true and recommend adding a focused unit test for the target symbol — either before the patch (test-first) or alongside it. Do NOT recommend running the entire test suite as the sole option.

args.recommendation_template: Add tests/unit/test_${symbol}.py covering the primary branches of ${symbol} before applying the patch.

failure_policy.notes: This step is advisory; if it can't produce a recommendation, surface coverage_gap=true and stop.

success_criteria: A targeted test file or suggested_command is recorded, OR coverage_gap=true is reported with a recommended_test_path
The workflow does not fall back to a project-wide pytest as the only recommendation unless every other step produced empty results
When suggested_commands are surfaced, they are kept as targeted invocations (e.g. `pytest tests/unit/test_X.py`), not whole-suite runs
next_actions: Run omni_impact(symbol='${symbol}', format='json') to pull the suggested-tests list
Then omni_search(query='test ${symbol}', mode='hybrid', around_file='tests/', format='json') to catch dynamic-dispatch tests
If both steps produced empty results, follow the suggest_new_test step's template and add a focused unit test before patching
If you found tests, run the targeted suggested_commands (and only fall back to the full suite if they pass)
